rationale: The citing paper references Hagtvedt and Patrick (2008) multiple times in core theory sections, not just in a bibliography-style list. It is used as prior empirical evidence of positive contagion effects and to characterize the prevailing view that contagion depends on a source-target link and can persist. Those points are central to motivating the paper's contribution that emotions from persuasion can spill over to unrelated nearby evaluations via affect-as-information.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as part of the conceptual and empirical base for contagion effects in persuasion-related contexts. It then builds from that baseline by proposing and testing contagion to unrelated products and brands, rather than only linked source-target pairings.
